corpus.name: Western Romantic Symphony (late 19th c.)
corpus.notes: Anchor for dense Western tonal polyphony. High voice_count + voice_independence, strict notation, pronounced arc. Pitch system is dense-ish only because 12 is more than 5-7; calibrate pitch_density against non-Western systems below.

corpus.name: Delta Blues (solo voice + guitar)
corpus.notes: texture_type=monophonic is arguable (voice + guitar) but the guitar doubles/comments the voice rather than providing independent lines — closer to monophonic-with-accompaniment than true polyphony. Blue-note bending drives microtonality_degree above zero.

corpus.name: Hindustani Khayal (North Indian classical vocal)
corpus.notes: Anchor for long-form, improvisation-dominant, drone-foundation systems. Rhythmic_structure varies across sections (alap=pulse_free, jhala=isochronous_metric); I'm encoding the dominant alap-jor character since that's what defines the raga experience. interval_hierarchy near max due to vadi/samvadi.

corpus.name: Balinese Gamelan Gong Kebyar
corpus.notes: 5-tone pelog/slendro → very low pitch_density. Inharmonic bronze overtones are why tuning_basis=inharmonic_spectral rather than just_intonation. interlocking_parts (kotekan) is the defining texture. Intense tempo changes within a piece temper tempo_stability below 1.0.

corpus.name: Gregorian Plainchant
corpus.notes: Monophonic unison choir — voice_count = 0.1 reflects multiple singers at unison, voice_independence = 0.0 because they're singing the same line. Pulse_free word-rhythm. notated_skeletal = neumes give contour but not precise rhythm.

corpus.name: Japanese Gagaku (tōgaku court music)
corpus.notes: 'Floating pulse' is captured by low metric_periodicity + low tempo_stability. Shō mouth-organ cluster chords push drone_presence upward; these aren't a single drone but sustained tone-clusters that function like one. Anchor for 'static-contemplative non-Western ensemble'.

corpus.name: Ewe Agbekor (West African drum ensemble)
corpus.notes: Anchor for max polyrhythmic_complexity. Pitch system is minimal (drums have relative pitch; master drum cues set hierarchy); octave_equivalence is meaningless on unpitched drums so I've set it low. Bell timeline structure → single_cycle_no_repeat scale_topology (the repeating unit isn't pitch-based).

corpus.name: Tuvan Khoomei (throat singing — sygyt style)
corpus.notes: Anchor for overtone-based pitch systems. Single performer produces drone + melody from upper harmonics → voice_independence = 0.5 because the two streams (fundamental drone + whistle melody) come from one body. Pitch density counts accessible overtones as distinct pitches.

corpus.name: Inuit Katajjaq (throat-singing game)
corpus.notes: Anchor for breath-based rhythmic_structure. Pieces end when one performer laughs or runs out of breath → event_duration temporal_framing. Voiced/unvoiced sounds dominate over pitch; pitch_density near zero. Rare combination: high rhythmic_density + breath-based + non-pitched.

corpus.name: Aboriginal Australian Song Cycle (Dreaming)
corpus.notes: Anchor for geographic_mapped temporal_framing — songs map to land features and the cycle proceeds in geographic order. Piece_duration = 1.0 because full cycles can span days. Didgeridoo provides drone; clapsticks provide ritual_cued pulse.

corpus.name: Carnatic Ragam Tanam Pallavi (South Indian classical)
corpus.notes: Pairs with Hindustani khayal but differs: higher pitch_density (22-shruti system more strictly enforced), higher rhythmic_density in tanam and pallavi sections, more complex tala cycles. Ornament_density = 0.95 (gamaka is nearly continuous).

corpus.name: Aka Pygmy Forest Polyphony
corpus.notes: Anchor for communal improvised polyphony with no composer. Every singer chooses their own line within a shared tonal field → high voice_independence + high improvisation simultaneously (one of the rare combinations flagged in coherence_constraints soft_warnings). Yodeling hocket drives ornament_grammar.

corpus.name: Persian Dastgah (classical Iranian music)
corpus.notes: Radif is a corpus of memorized gushehs, arranged in performance — improvisation_degree is 0.7 not higher because structure comes from the memorized radif. Quarter-tone koron and sori drive microtonality.

corpus.name: Arabic Maqam (Egyptian takht ensemble)
corpus.notes: Sits close to Persian dastgah but differs: ensemble-based (higher voice_count), more metric (iqaʿat rhythmic cycles), longer compositional forms (wasla), entertainment_display over contemplative framing (tarab is audience-interactive). soft_warning for ensemble microtonality applies.

corpus.name: Free Jazz Ensemble (post-Coltrane small group)
corpus.notes: Anchor for maximum ensemble-improvisation combination. interval_hierarchy near zero (no fixed tonal center). microtonality is high due to expressive bending, not a fixed microtonal tuning → tuning_basis=equal_temperament is a reasonable default. Scale_topology=octave_repeating because the underlying pitch vocabulary is 12-TET even when improvisers bend.

corpus.name: Steve Reich Phase-Process Minimalism
corpus.notes: Anchor for max metric_periodicity + max tempo_stability + zero ornamentation. Soft_warning active: piece_duration 0.55 + metric_periodicity 1.0 (long + strict). Formal_type=modular_combinatorial captures phase-process structure better than sectional_contrasting.

corpus.name: Spectral Composition (French school)
corpus.notes: Anchor for non_octave_repeating + overtone_series + spectral_cloud. Pitch material comes from analyzing acoustic spectra — weakens octave_equivalence but doesn't eliminate it (some pieces retain it). indeterminate_proportional rhythm: precisely notated durations but no shared pulse.

corpus.name: Shakuhachi Honkyoku (Zen flute solo)
corpus.notes: Anchor for 'breath is the form' + silence as equal-to-sound. Historically honkyoku is suizen (blowing Zen) — played as religious practice, not performance → listener_role=no_listener_intended. Each phrase = one breath; form = sequence of breaths. Heavy microtonal pitch-bending (meri/kari) without fixed microtonal scale.

corpus.name: Bulgarian Women's Choir (Koutev / Mystère des voix bulgares)
corpus.notes: Anchor for additive_metric (7+7+9, 11+11+13 etc.). Drone_presence 0.5 because ison (held tone) is common but not constant. Voice_independence is moderate — parts move in parallel seconds often (that dissonant 'bulgarian second' sound).

corpus.name: Sardinian Tenores (four-voice male polyphony)
corpus.notes: Archaic European polyphony anchor. Four voices (bassu, contra, mesu oche, oche) — bassu and contra produce throat-harmonic overtones that are the defining sound. drone_presence is high because bassu/contra function as a chord-drone. Notable because it's a pre-classical polyphony that ignores the Franco-Flemish counterpoint tradition.

corpus.name: Bach Fugue (Well-Tempered Clavier)
corpus.notes: Solo keyboard fugue — voice_count low because single instrument, but voice_independence near max (4 real voices). didactic_pedagogical because WTC is explicitly pedagogical but also cosmologically ordered.

corpus.name: Mozart Classical Piano Sonata
corpus.notes: Calibrates mid-range Western tonal solo against symphony/Bach. Lower pitch_flexibility than Romantic era; lower ornament than Baroque ornament tradition would have.

corpus.name: Wagnerian Opera (Music-Drama)
corpus.notes: piece_duration near max — 4 operas, ~15 hours. Leitmotif construction pushes development_arc very high. Voice_count large orchestra + singers.

corpus.name: Schoenberg 12-Tone Composition
corpus.notes: interval_hierarchy near zero (all 12 tones equal by construction — a defining feature). Sprechstimme pushes pitch_flexibility modestly above Bach/Mozart.

corpus.name: Cage Chance-Operation Composition
corpus.notes: Notated prescriptive (I-Ching derived scores) but content is chance-generated. development_arc near zero — anti-arc. silence_treatment=equal_to_sound is the Cage signature.

corpus.name: Stockhausen Gruppen (spatial composition)
corpus.notes: Anchor for spatially_distributed ensemble_topology — three physically separated orchestras with independent conductors. Polyrhythmic_complexity near max from three tempo streams.

corpus.name: Feldman Late Chamber Work
corpus.notes: piece_duration high (80 min+), rhythmic_density very low, development_arc near zero. static_contemplation is the canonical fit. ornament_grammar=forbidden (Feldman's bare surface).

corpus.name: Xenakis Stochastic Composition
corpus.notes: Mass sonic events generated by probability distributions. texture_type=spectral_cloud fits — glissando masses, stochastic clouds. pitch_density very high.

corpus.name: La Monte Young Drone (Well-Tuned Piano)
corpus.notes: Just-intonation extended to complex rational-ratio pitch grid. Well-Tuned Piano performances run 5+ hours. drone_presence absolute; piece_duration max. Anchor for contemporary drone art.

corpus.name: Éliane Radigue Electronic Drone
corpus.notes: ARP 2500 synthesizer drones. Recorded fixed works. Sits near La Monte Young but electronic transmission_mode and shorter piece_duration.

corpus.name: Swing-Era Big Band
corpus.notes: Arranged sections + solo improvisation. Notated prescriptive arrangements + improvised solos — a hybrid the transmission_mode can't fully capture. Calibrates ensemble popular-music region.

corpus.name: Bebop Jazz Combo
corpus.notes: Max rhythmic_density — bebop eighth-note phrase. Head-solos-head form. transmission_mode=recorded_reference because lineage is via recordings + transcription.

corpus.name: Classic Rock Song
corpus.notes: Verse-chorus structure → formal_repetition high. Anchor for Western popular song format.

corpus.name: Four-on-the-Floor Techno
corpus.notes: DJ-as-composer — solo ensemble_topology. Kick drum on every beat. Additive layer structure (modular_combinatorial). social_function=trance_induction through sustained repetition + physical dance.

corpus.name: Brian Eno Ambient
corpus.notes: Eno's 'as ignorable as it is interesting.' listener_role=environmental_coexistent is the Eno invention. Tape-loop systems produce layered_ostinato without strict meter.

corpus.name: Drone/Doom Metal (Sunn O))))
corpus.notes: Extreme-volume distorted-guitar drone. Ritual-performance aesthetic but framed as art. drone_presence near max; pitch_flexibility from feedback/overtones.

corpus.name: Hyperpop
corpus.notes: Max rhythmic_density; pitched vocals heavily pitch-corrected and pitch_flexibility is ornamental rather than expressive. Piece_duration very short (2-3 min).

corpus.name: IDM / Glitch
corpus.notes: Algorithmic/granular manipulation. Polyrhythmic_complexity high from non-integer-ratio rhythmic events. Close to Cage + spectral lineage but electronic.

corpus.name: Appalachian Old-Time String Band
corpus.notes: Fiddle + banjo + guitar playing variations of same tune simultaneously — heterophonic texture. Dance function drives metric stability. Short tunes (AABB form).

corpus.name: Irish Session (trad. session playing)
corpus.notes: Ornamentation (rolls, cuts, crans) obligatory for idiomatic playing. Pitch_flexibility higher than Appalachian due to modal scale inflections. Pipes drone influence on just intonation.

corpus.name: Sea Shanty (work song)
corpus.notes: Pure work_coordination — tempo matches task (hauling rhythm). Shantyman leads verse, crew answers chorus. Ends when work ends.

corpus.name: English Folk Ballad (Child ballad tradition)
corpus.notes: Solo narrative ballad, strophic. Calibrates 'unaccompanied folk song' against American solo blues (which has guitar).

corpus.name: Sufi Qawwali (Pakistan/India)
corpus.notes: Anchor for trance_induction. Handclaps + harmonium + lead + chorus. Pieces accelerate to ecstatic climax over 20-40 min. Devotional but listener participation is central.

corpus.name: Tabla Solo (North Indian percussion)
corpus.notes: Tabla over sarangi/harmonium lehara drone. cycle_length moderate-high (often 16-beat teental). didactic because solos explicitly demonstrate compositions from the memorized tabla repertoire.

corpus.name: Bollywood Filmi Song (golden era)
corpus.notes: Prescriptive orchestral arrangement + raga/ornament-inflected vocal melody. Calibrates 'Indian-influenced ensemble film song' against Western pop.

corpus.name: Turkish Classical Makam
corpus.notes: Sibling to Arabic maqam and Persian dastgah. Longer usul rhythmic cycles (additive_metric, e.g. aksak 9/8). 53-tone Turkish theory → highest systematic pitch_density among Near Eastern traditions.

corpus.name: Flamenco Cante Jondo
corpus.notes: Vocal front-and-center with guitar. Compás (12-beat additive) = additive_metric. Cante jondo is the deep-song branch — extreme emotional weight, Phrygian-inflected.

corpus.name: Greek Rebetiko
corpus.notes: Urban tavern music — Ottoman-inflected scales (dromoi) in Balkan idiom. Zeibekiko rhythm = 9/4 additive. Songs of the manges subculture.

corpus.name: Sephardic Liturgical Song
corpus.notes: Cross-Mediterranean oral tradition, modal, strophic. Differs from Sephardic secular (more dance-meter).

corpus.name: Byzantine Orthodox Chant
corpus.notes: Ison (held drone) + chanted melody. Octoechos = eight modes. Microtonality_degree higher than Gregorian because of modal inflections. neumatic notated_skeletal.

corpus.name: Chinese Guqin (scholar's zither)
corpus.notes: Silk-string zither played for one's own cultivation — no_listener_intended. Tablature notates fingerings but not rhythm. Microtonal slides (naoyin, yinyin) drive pitch_flexibility near max.

corpus.name: Korean Pansori (solo epic narrative)
corpus.notes: Singer + single drummer, performances 3-8 hours. Pansori jangdan are additive rhythmic cycles. Voice includes extreme guttural/speech/sung inflections → pitch_flexibility max.

corpus.name: Korean Samulnori (four-drum ensemble)
corpus.notes: Four percussion instruments (kkwaenggwari, jing, janggu, buk) in interlocking rhythmic cycles. Concert form derived from p'ungmul village tradition. Tempo accelerations produce arc.

corpus.name: Korean Aak Court Music
corpus.notes: Anchor for notated_prescriptive non-Western ensemble. Preserved via Akhakgwebeom (15th c. treatise). Slow, ceremonial Confucian ritual music with large ensemble including dancers. Rare extant East Asian prescriptive notation tradition.

corpus.name: Peking Opera (jingju)
corpus.notes: Stylized vocal types (dan, sheng, etc.) with fixed ornament grammars. Jinghu fiddle + percussion ensemble. Character-type vocal style is the primary ornament system.

corpus.name: Mongolian Urtiin Duu (long song)
corpus.notes: Single-syllable phrases extended over long melismatic breaths. Ornament_density near max (nugulalt trills). Traditionally sung to horses/the steppe — environmental_dialogue.

corpus.name: Japanese Noh Theatre Music
corpus.notes: Chorus + shite (principal actor) + hayashi (drums + flute). Jo-ha-kyū structure (slow-break-rapid). Extremely slow overall tempo; ornament-dense vocal style.

corpus.name: Javanese Gamelan (Central Javanese court)
corpus.notes: Slower, more meditative than Balinese gong kebyar. Longer gong cycles (cycle_length higher). Gendhing form emphasizes gong structure over interlocking density. Pairs with balinese_gong_kebyar as contrast.

corpus.name: Vietnamese Ca Trù
corpus.notes: Female singer + đàn đáy lute + phách (bamboo clapper). Extremely ornamented vocal style. Near-extinct UNESCO-listed tradition.

corpus.name: Zulu Isicathamiya (a cappella choral)
corpus.notes: Male a cappella choral tradition emerged from migrant hostel culture. Soft-stepping dance — isicathamiya means 'stalk'. Homophonic four-part texture.

corpus.name: Mbira dzavadzimu (Shona spirit music)
corpus.notes: All-night bira ceremonies to summon ancestors. Two mbiras play interlocking kushaura and kutsinhira parts. Formal_repetition near max but formal_type=cyclical_return with gradual variation (kutema).

corpus.name: Moroccan Gnawa Lila (all-night trance)
corpus.notes: Gap-filler #1: canonical trance_induction tradition. Maâlem leads guimbri + qraqeb castanets + chorus. All-night lila ritual to summon spirits. Each mluk (spirit) has specific colors + tonal framework. piece_duration very high (single suite 30-90 min; whole lila 8+ hrs).

corpus.name: Ethiopian Orthodox Zema
corpus.notes: Three melodic modes (Ge'ez, Ezel, Araray). Yared's melekket notation system is unique to Ethiopia. Debtera chanters + sistrum (tsenatsil) + drums.

corpus.name: Manding Kora Tradition (jeli)
corpus.notes: 21-string harp-lute. Jeli (griot) plays kumbengo (ostinato) + birimintingo (improvised runs) simultaneously — hence voice_independence via one instrument. Narrative function — historical epics.

corpus.name: Central African Horn/Flute Ensemble (hocket)
corpus.notes: Each player sounds a single pitch — melody emerges ONLY from group hocket. Extreme example of interlocking_parts + voice_independence without individual ornament.

corpus.name: Navajo Diné Ceremonial Song Cycle
corpus.notes: Multi-day ceremonies (Nightway, Blessingway). Text-melody correspondence is cosmologically load-bearing — errors invalidate the ceremony. Rattle/drum drive metric pulse.

corpus.name: Andean Quechua Wayno
corpus.notes: Pentatonic core + Spanish colonial influence. Quena flute, charango, drum. Seasonal festival function → natural_cycle_aligned temporal_framing.

corpus.name: Native American Peyote Chant (NAC)
corpus.notes: All-night ceremony, 4 ritual songs from each participant in turn. Water drum + gourd rattle fix tempo. Set of songs per participant → piece_duration structural.

corpus.name: Haitian Vodou Rada Drumming
corpus.notes: Three-drum ensemble (manman, seconde, boula) + ogan bell. Master drummer (manman) improvises against fixed second/third drum patterns. Drives possession trance.

corpus.name: Cuban Son Montuno
corpus.notes: Clave-based rhythmic framework. Verse + montuno (call-response vamp) structure. Anchor for clave-based Afro-Cuban forms; reggaeton and salsa descend from here.

corpus.name: Brazilian Samba Escola Parade
corpus.notes: Second spatially_distributed example — parade stretches over 800m with 3000+ percussionists. Bateria sections in interlocking patterns. Samba-enredo melody sung simultaneously.

corpus.name: Black Gospel (Pentecostal tradition)
corpus.notes: Altar-call gospel aims for ecstatic climax → arc_buildup + trance_induction. Melisma-heavy lead + call-response with congregation. Pitch_flexibility max (shouts, runs).

corpus.name: Argentine Tango
corpus.notes: Orquesta típica: bandoneones + strings + piano + bass. Rubato (hence tempo_stability moderate). Prescriptive arrangements, but bandoneon ornaments add flex.

corpus.name: Mexican Mariachi
corpus.notes: Trumpets, violins, vihuela, guitarrón, vocals. Son jalisciense base. Strophic song form. Functional at weddings, funerals, serenatas — life_cycle_event.

corpus.name: Reggaeton (dembow-based)
corpus.notes: Dembow riddim = near-universal rhythmic template. Producer-as-author. Low formal_repetition for verse/chorus → high. Descends from son clave via dancehall.

corpus.name: Gyütö Tantric Throat Chant
corpus.notes: Monks sing ultra-low fundamentals producing audible 5th/10th harmonics — overtone melody without soloistic line. Anchor for collective overtone-chord singing (contrast solo Tuvan).

corpus.name: Shaker Hymnody (Millennial Church)
corpus.notes: Monophonic (Shakers rejected harmony as worldly). ornament_grammar=forbidden (doctrinal simplicity). Unison communal song with dance. letter-notation system unique to the sect.

corpus.name: Hasidic Nigun (wordless melody)
corpus.notes: Wordless devotional melody sung communally, often with gradual acceleration to ecstatic climax. Lubavitch 'deveikus' nigunim — meditative tempo. Distinct from qawwali/gnawa by monophonic + no drum.

corpus.name: Aboriginal Australian Didgeridoo Solo
corpus.notes: Circular breathing produces continuous drone; vocalizations + tongue/cheek articulations modulate overtones. Part of ceremony but here framed as solo instrumental tradition.

corpus.name: Hawaiian Oli (chant)
corpus.notes: Unaccompanied chant preserving genealogies, cosmogony, place-names. Very narrow pitch range but intricate i‛i (trill) ornaments on a small number of tones.

corpus.name: Lachenmann Musique Concrète Instrumentale
corpus.notes: Extended instrumental techniques (bowing the body, key clicks, breath noises) composed into concrete-music forms. Pitch is present but not primary — texture/noise is the content.

corpus.name: Tony Conrad Violin Drone
corpus.notes: Just-intonation bowed-string drones emphasizing beating patterns. Formative pre-La Monte Young Theatre of Eternal Music. Shorter duration than Well-Tuned Piano.

corpus.name: Inuit Drum-Dance Song
corpus.notes: Different from katajjaq — this is solo sung narrative with frame drum (qilaut). Pisiit are 'personal songs' composed for specific life events.

corpus.name: Sardinian Launeddas (triple clarinet)
corpus.notes: One player, three pipes — drone pipe + tumbu (low melody) + mancosedda (high counter-melody). Circular breathing. voice_independence moderate because one person plays three simultaneous parts.
